content: Evaluate the number and quality of polymarket data related with gold price prediction

args.thought: Plan: Evaluate Polymarket gold data quality. I need to (1) search Polymarket for active gold markets, (2) assess market liquidity/volume, (3) check if the probabilities cited in the previous analysis (8.9% below $3,400, 25.5% above $5,500 for June 2026) actually exist, (4) compare with the prediction markets block shown at top of prompt (which shows 0% for $3,000/$6,000 by end of March), and (5) provide an honest assessment of data reliability for gold price prediction.

No active Polymarket prediction markets found for "XAU". Try broader keywords.

args.thought: Assess data quality: I have 10 gold markets. Key observations: (1) Liquidity ranges from $1K-$277K, with most markets under $35K - this is THIN. (2) 24h volume ranges $1K-$182K, again mostly under $30K. (3) The $3,000 March market has best liquidity ($277K) but expires in 2 days (March 31). (4) June markets ($3,400 low, $5,500/$7,000/$8,000 high) have only $10K-$26K liquidity - too thin for reliable probability signals. (5) No XAU-ticker markets found. (6) Comparison: these volumes are 10-100x smaller than typical political/election markets on Polymarket. Verdict: Data exists but quality is LOW for serious prediction work - thin liquidity means prices can be manipulated or stale.

Final assessment: I need to synthesize findings into a clear verdict. Key points: (1) 10 gold markets exist but liquidity is thin, (2) Best market is $3,000 March ($277K liq) but expires in 2 days, (3) June markets have only $10K-$26K liquidity - unreliable for probability signals, (4) No year-end 2026 markets found, (5) Volumes are 10-100x smaller than political markets, (6) Recommendation: Use analyst consensus ($4,742-$5,686/oz) as primary signal, treat Polymarket as secondary/noise for gold.
